title: AI Service Provider Bootstrap
activity_flow: ACTION: app->scoped(AiManager::class, fn => new AiManager($app))  [scoped singleton]
NOTE: ChatCommand is commented out / disabled
ACTION: publishes agent.stub, structured-agent.stub, tool.stub, agent-middleware.stub → base_path('stubs/')  [tag: 'ai-stubs']
Macro body: builds Embeddings request, optionally sets dimensions/cache/timeout, calls generate(provider, model)
Macro body: resolves items via Closure|array|string, calls Reranking::of()->limit()->rerank(), maps results by index
key_relationships: AiManager uses traits: InteractsWithFakeAgents, InteractsWithFakeAudio, InteractsWithFakeEmbeddings, InteractsWithFakeFiles, InteractsWithFakeImages, InteractsWithFakeReranking, InteractsWithFakeStores, InteractsWithFakeTranscriptions
AiServiceProvider registers AiManager as scoped (per-request) singleton
AiServiceProvider registers ConversationStore → DatabaseConversationStore as singleton
All other drivers (Azure, Cohere, DeepSeek, ElevenLabs, Groq, Jina, Mistral, Ollama, OpenRouter, VoyageAI, xAI) → Provider(config, Dispatcher)
branching_conditions: boot(): if app->runningInConsole() → register commands + publishable resources
MakeAgentCommand::getStub(): if option('structured') → structured-agent.stub else agent.stub
MakeAgentCommand::afterPromptingForMissingArguments(): if didReceiveOptions() → return early, else prompt confirm for --structured
AiManager provider methods: if instance does not implement required interface → throw LogicException
AiManager fakeable* methods: if capability is faked → clone provider and inject fake gateway, else return real provider
AiManager::getDefaultInstance(): if default is Lab enum → use ->value, else use raw string
AiManager::getInstanceConfig(): if config driver is Lab enum → convert to ->value
important_notes: AiManager is scoped (not a global singleton) — one instance per HTTP request lifecycle
ChatCommand exists but is commented out in registerCommands()
Stringable and Collection macros are always registered, regardless of console context
Config key is 'ai'; default provider read from ai.default; provider configs from ai.providers.<name>
Lab is an enum used as a provider identifier; driver values are normalized via ->value before use
Stub resolution: checks app basePath first (publishable override), falls back to package stubs
